lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Tiffy, I lost my CommOrb...

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Very careless of you, what? Here, take another, but be careful of this one!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Good day, Proselyte. I heard from Akrisae what happened with that Lucien business. Be sure to keep your chin up. We'll get the old boy sooner or later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, Tiffy.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Don't mention it, old bean. Now, is there anything I can do for you?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Good day Proselyte. Is there anything I can do for you?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Sorry old bean but we are still in the process of organising.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: I'm sure that we will have something for you soon, so please feel free to check back later. Anything else I can do for you in the meantime?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have news on that assassin who attacked Entrana.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Ah yes, you've been helping out the Ardougne authorities I hear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You knew?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Of course! Knowledge is my business, old bean, and it pays to know what's happening across the other kingdoms.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Now, as for your assassin, quite messy stuff, what? We've been trying to track him down ever since the Entrana incident, but he's proven quite the tricky one to pin down. Curious him showing up in Ardougne.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are your plans?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Sadly, very little. Until we can learn more about what he's up to, we don't have much to go on. You have nothing to fear though, we'll find him sooner or later!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Now, anything else you need, [milady/sirrah]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well did you also know he was a Mahjarrat?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: A Mahjarrat! You must be pulling on my leg, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: His name is Sliske. Have you heard of him?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Sliske, hmm? We have dossiers on many Mahjarrat, but I must confess, this is a new name to me. Poor show! I'll have to see what we have hiding in the archives.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: While you're there, it might be worth looking up something called the Elder Horn. It's an artefact that Sliske has now claimed. One that has the ability to control minds.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: A mind controlling artefact you say? Sounds like I'll need to report this to the top brass. Can't see them being too thrilled about it. Thank you for the update, old bean.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: A mind controlling artefact you say? First Lucien and the Stone, and now this! The top brass will need to be made aware. Can't see them being too thrilled about it. Thank you for the update, old bean.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Now, anything else you need, [milady/sirrah]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't really understand this 'Gaze of Saradomin' thing... Do you think you could explain what it does for me?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Certainly [milady/sirrah]! As you know, we Temple Knights are personally favoured by Saradomin himself.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: And when I say personally favoured, I don't mean that sometime off in the future he's going to buy us all a drink!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: He watches over each of us, and when we die he catches us as we fall, and ensures we arrive back at Falador castle safe and sound.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: We usually lose some equipment when he does so, but it's a small price to pay to be hale and hearty again, what?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Some lucky fellows have a similar system going already, but when they die they spawn in that squalid little swamp village Lumbridge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, what kind of person would want to spawn there... Certainly not me, and I never have! Honest!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well, you should be glad that we offer you a step up then! Falador is clearly a far superior town to spend your time in!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Was there something else you wanted to ask good old Tiffy, [milady/sirrah]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I buy some armour?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Of course old bean. I can sell you up to Proselyte level items only I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I switch respawns, please?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Ah, so you'd like to respawn in Falador, the good old homestead! Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I want to respawn in Falador.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Top-hole, what? Good old Fally is definitely the hot-spot nowadays!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, no thanks. I like my respawn point.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: As you wish, what? Ta-ta for now.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: What? You're saying you want to respawn in Lumbridge? Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I want to respawn in Lumbridge.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Why anyone would want to visit that smelly little swamp village of oiks is quite beyond me, I'm afraid, but the deed is done now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, no thanks. I like my respawn point.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: As you wish, what? Ta-ta for now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, see you around Tiffy.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Ta-ta for now, old bean!

sections.title: If the notes were not opened

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sir Tiffy! I got this scroll from a knight near Trollheim. He said I should deliver it to you urgently.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: What ho, old [boy/gal/bean]. Well, hand it over and let's have a look, shall we?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Now, let's see what is so urgent. Hmmm...

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well, you've been a great help, so I'll tell you a bit about this, shall I? Several weeks ago, a dwarven explorer, named Nestor Peregrine, discovered the entrance to an ancient cave complex while exploring the

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: troll mountains. On his return to Keldagrim, he celebrated in a tavern where we have an informant. The dwarf spoke of a great treasure, apparently mentioned in dwarven legend, and of creatures of great

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: evil, fighting those of great good. I sent Sir Gerry and a small command of knights to investigate the dwarf's stories several months ago and it appears that he has found the resting place of that incredible weapon: the

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Godsword.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Godsword? What's that?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: During the great God Wars, the evil usurper Zamorak gathered many ancient relics and armies to his cause. There were none powerful enough to stand alone before his might, so our glorious Lord Saradomin

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: gathered a triumverate of gods to stem the tide of evil. They came together and, using great magics, they created the Godsword; a weapon so mighty that it could slay even a god. It was forged in the flames of a

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: volcano and tempered by the tears of the icyene. The mightiest warriors to stand before the army of evil were the aviansie: birdmen followers of Armadyl. They were a proud and mighty warrior race. To them fell the task of

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: delivering the Godsword to the holy armies that gathered to oppose Zamorak's evil. Unfortunately, they were attacked by an army of demons before they could reach the safety of the main army. It was a fiendish

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: stroke by the forces of darkness. At the same time as they attacked the aviansie, they also mounted a huge attack on the main armies that opposed them. Realising their intent, several of the gods that were allied against

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Zamorak sent forces to aid the besieged aviansie, who had retreated into a temple in the mountains. The forces of Saradomin and Bandos arrived in the midst of a pitched battle for the Godsword, and it was during this

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: battle that the sword was sundered. Shortly thereafter, the evil one cast a spell of such devastation that it flattened the entire northern half of Gielinor, reducing it to what we now call the Wilderness. This temple must

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: have collapsed, sealing the combatants in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Surely, one of the groups would have gained the upper hand by now? After all, it's been thousands of years since that battle.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Apparently not. It appears that a millennia-old battle is still being fought beneath the ruins of the temple, and the prize for winning the battle is the Godsword! Hmm... Well, thank you very much, old [boy/gal/bean]. You've been a

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: great help. I shall pass it on to my superiors.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that it, then? Aren't we going to do something?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Yes, yes. All in good time. I'll have to consult my commanding officer first. Thank you, old [boy/gal/bean], you're dismissed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sir Tiffy! I got this scroll from a knight near Trollheim. He said I should deliver it to you urgently.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: What ho, old [boy/gal/bean]. Well, hand it over and let's have a look, shall we?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Now, let's see what is so urgent. Hmm... I see that this scroll has been tampered with! The wax seal is broken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, yes. It was that way when I got it.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: I see. Anyway, let's see what it says.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Oh dear. Well, thank you very much, old [boy/gal/bean]. You've been a great help. I shall pass it on to my superiors.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that it then? Aren't we going to do something?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Yes, yes. All in good time. I'll have to consult my commanding officer first. Thank you, old [boy/gal/bean], you're dismissed.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Ah, you must be one of us. I must share this information with you: 40,41;1,6;49;12;1,2;37,41;45;89,75;16,20;103
